breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome Refraction Fix Makes Eclipse Geometry Incomputable — Model's Own Docs Admit Moon Position Unknown
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: New section 1.8.1 documents that the dome's aetheric refraction fix (introduced to prevent the sun colliding with the firmament) creates a trilemma: sun and moon are at different physical heights traversing different n(r) profiles, so eclipse alignment is geometrically indeterminate. The dome's own OPEN-007 explicitly labels moon mechanics a "structural problem" and the physical altitude "unknown." Yet the model claims 9 eclipse-related WINs and stakes a 2026 falsification test on this broken geometry.
breaking_news_suggestions.why_newsworthy: New standalone argument that independently invalidates 9 eclipse WINs; dome author's own documentation used as primary evidence; directly undermines 2026 falsification test
